questions.text: According to the Impact/Effort Matrix, which type of AI use case should you tackle first?

options.text: Strategic Projects — they have the biggest long-term payoff
Quick Wins — high impact, low effort tasks that build momentum
Deferred — they're the most technically interesting
Self-Service — they're the easiest to set up

questions.text: What does the RTF framework stand for in prompt engineering?

options.text: Review, Test, Finalise
Research, Target, Format
Role, Task, Format
Result, Tone, Frequency

questions.text: Which of these prompts would give the BEST result for a NZ business?

options.text: Write me a social media post.
Write a marketing post about my business for Facebook.
Act as a social media manager for a NZ plumbing company. Write a Facebook post promoting our winter heating check service. Under 150 words, friendly tone, include a call to action. NZ English.
I need help with social media. Can you generate some content?

questions.text: Which of the following are good characteristics of a FIRST AI use case? (Select all that apply)

options.text: It's a task you do frequently (daily or weekly)
It requires building a custom AI model
It's low-risk if something goes wrong
It involves sensitive client financial data
It's something that currently takes you significant time
Asking the AI the same question multiple times to get better answers
Giving the AI an example of the output you want before asking it to produce something new
Using short, minimal prompts to save time
Limiting the AI to only a few words in its response

questions.text: Which of these are AI features available in Xero's JAX? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Smart transaction reconciliation that learns from your patterns
Automatic tax filing and compliance decisions
Receipt capture from photos with automatic data extraction
Cash flow projections up to 180 days ahead
Replacing your accountant for end-of-year tax returns

questions.text: A business owner asks AI to write marketing copy. What is the MOST important thing they should do before publishing it?

options.text: Nothing — AI-generated content is always accurate and ready to publish
Post it immediately to save time, and fix any issues later
Review the content, verify any claims, add personal touches, and ensure it complies with the Fair Trading Act
Run it through a second AI tool to double-check it

questions.text: You use AI to analyse your monthly revenue data and it suggests a specific tax strategy. What should you do?

options.text: Follow the suggestion immediately — AI is trained on financial regulations
Ignore the analysis entirely — AI can't handle financial data
Use the analysis to understand your trends, but verify the tax advice with your accountant before acting on it
Ask the AI to recalculate with more data to confirm the strategy
